# Regression Safety Checklist: Product-Line Switcher and activeWorkspace Scoping

**Purpose**: Validate flag-off parity, flag-on Facility aggregate safety, grep guardrails, and SPEC-002-only test scope before task generation
**Created**: 2026-04-26
**Feature**: [Link to spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Flag-Off Regression Contract

- [x] CHK001 Are flag-off requirements defined as byte-compatible existing behavior rather than only hiding the switcher UI? [Completeness, Spec FR-001, Constitution I]
- [x] CHK002 Are flag-off regression requirements measurable for current test-count parity and Playwright snapshot parity, not just general behavior preservation? [Resolved in Spec SC-016, Plan Regression Safety Gates, Quickstart Feature-Flag Baseline Check]
- [x] CHK003 Is feature-flag hard-default-OFF behavior specified through `resolveFlag()` and workspace JSON opt-in rather than env-force-ON behavior? [Completeness, Spec FR-002, Research Decision 1]

## Flag-On Facility Baseline

- [x] CHK004 Are Facility aggregate semantics distinguished from selecting the real `facility` workspace row? [Completeness, Spec FR-003, FR-004, FR-011]
- [x] CHK005 Are flag-ON Facility regression requirements clear that existing baseline tests remain unchanged while new tests assert only Facility aggregate semantics? [Resolved in Spec User Story 2, SC-017, Plan Regression Safety Gates]
- [x] CHK006 Are Product Line and Facility request/cache/URL ownership requirements expressed through `scopeKey` so stale results cannot bleed across scopes? [Completeness, Spec FR-009, SC-014]

## Test Scope Boundaries

- [x] CHK007 Are new-test requirements constrained to SPEC-002 behavior so tasks cannot encode SPEC-003+ assumptions? [Resolved in Spec Deferred Boundaries, Plan Regression Safety Gates, Quickstart SPEC-002-Only Test Boundary]
- [x] CHK008 Are skills and local/gateway sessions/transcripts explicitly kept Facility/global rather than Product Line-owned for this phase? [Consistency, Spec FR-012, Panel Taxonomy]
- [x] CHK009 Is multi-facility tenant modeling explicitly excluded from SPEC-002 semantics? [Scope Control, Spec Assumptions]
- [x] CHK010 Are global/local agent duplicate-name requirements limited to id-based mutation safety without implementing downstream Aegis ownership semantics? [Scope Control, Spec FR-016]

## Static Guardrails

- [x] CHK011 Are grep guardrails specified for inline `process.env.FEATURE_*` reads outside `resolveFlag()`? [Resolved in Plan Regression Safety Gates, Quickstart Static Guardrail Checks]
- [x] CHK012 Are grep guardrails specified for new direct `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_*`, `config.gatewayHost`, `config.gatewayPort`, or `gateways.is_primary` assumptions outside approved resolver/adapter paths when gateway-facing code is touched? [Resolved in Plan Regression Safety Gates, Quickstart Static Guardrail Checks]
- [x] CHK013 Are runtime gateway/session/transcript surfaces prevented from becoming Product Line-routed in SPEC-002? [Scope Control, Spec FR-012, Panel Taxonomy]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK014 Can flag-off behavior, Playwright snapshots, and no-switcher-visible behavior be traced to success criteria? [Traceability, Spec SC-001, SC-010]
- [x] CHK015 Can Facility/Product Line authorization behavior be traced to explicit REST, URL, and SSE contract requirements? [Traceability, Spec FR-011, FR-015, FR-019]
- [x] CHK016 Does the plan keep remediation and implementation boundaries limited to generated artifacts and later SPEC-002 runtime work only? [Scope Control, Plan Strict Scope]

## Remediation Re-run

- [x] CHK017 Are all regression-safety gap markers from the initial run resolved in generated SPEC-002 artifacts? [Completeness, Spec SC-016, SC-017; Plan Regression Safety Gates]
- [x] CHK018 Does the rerun preserve the flag-OFF zero-regression contract without adding production runtime code? [Scope Control, Quickstart Feature-Flag Baseline Check]
- [x] CHK019 Does the rerun preserve flag-ON Facility aggregate semantics without rewriting baseline test expectations? [Scope Control, Spec SC-017]
- [x] CHK020 Does the rerun keep grep enforcement as requirements/verification guidance rather than implementation changes? [Scope Control, Quickstart Static Guardrail Checks]
- [x] CHK021 Does the rerun explicitly prevent SPEC-003+ behavior from entering SPEC-002 tests or tasks? [Scope Control, Spec Deferred Boundaries]
